title: ophrys umbilicata var. attica
intro: Ophrys umbilicata var. attica, also known as the Hoary Spotted Orchid, is a rare and endemic orchid species found in Greece. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Traditionally, Ophrys umbilicata var. attica has been used in folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it has been used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
